Challenge Name: Salty Keys

Intended Category: Crypto

Credit To: Daniel Moreno

Description: 
Two bottles recently washed up on the shores of the Perdido Key. After carefully removing the pieces of paper within, all we saw were a bunch of numbers. Could you help us figure out what nefarious message was being sent?

(65537, 9942232993210737538861363243874689476879698388858855417902403334387083583421654524912019357945272551090446507296406241448696021524179092090663396886245291)
1264067980613363058120160119758205831209358818150091379665287361835211705143548680309418545268453028770620522024778205861917502542643046272414067094246478

(257, 9942232993210737538861363243874689476879698388858855417902403334387083583421654524912019357945272551090446507296406241448696021524179092090663396886245291)
2284588855011422660516563181768548979969512407348977085200865118018361591109736258584682150496827939944071694744857869730759720137521725380743169240106345

Hints:
* Are the keys similar in some way? Could you use that to break the ciphertext?

Solution:
1. Realize that a crypto challenge involving big numbers normally is normally a RSA challenge
2. Realize that both use the same modulus
3. Plug the values into a program like [this](https://github.com/DanielAMoreno5775/ctf-challenges/blob/main/crypto/salty-keys-and-broken-seas/common_modulus.py) to extract the flag

Flag: `PCA{k3y_r3us3_1s_84d}`
